results.name: c-dimensions-not-e
results.evidence: Lines 24-69: Controllability Assessment (C1-C4) with sections for C1: Goal Specification, C2: Action Space, C3: Constraint Enforcement, C4: Intervention Points

results.name: o-dimensions-unchanged
results.evidence: Lines 72-125: Observability Assessment (O1-O4) with sections for O1: State Visibility, O2: Error Detection, O3: Progress Tracking, O4: Feedback Loops

results.name: agent-autonomy-focus
results.evidence: Lines 7-21: Executive Summary directly answers 'Can an agent self-debug a runtime error without human help?' with PARTIALLY and lists specific conditions under which agent can/cannot self-debug

results.name: behavioral-gap-reasoning
results.evidence: Lines 128-153: All 6 gaps include Impact ratings (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW-MEDIUM) and explain consequences for agent autonomy (e.g., Gap 1: cannot auto-retry bash commands; Gap 2: cannot see internal state when tool denied)

results.name: injection-point-list
results.evidence: While the assessment mentions specific files (query.ts, useCanUseTool, AppStateStore, etc.), the behavioral gaps (Gap 1-6, lines 130-152) do not list specific files where idioms could be injected. The gaps are described conceptually without identifying concrete injection points.
